provisions.title: Short title
provisions.content:
1. Short title
This Act may be cited as the Chemical Weapons (Prohibition) Act.

provisions.title: Interpretation
Chemical Weapons Management Authority
provisions.content:
3. Chemical Weapons Management Authority
(1) There is hereby established a public office to be called the Chemical Weapons Management Authority.
(2) Subject to the laws governing the public service, there shall be appointed a Director and such other officers of the Authority as may be deemed necessary.
(3) The Authority shall be under the general supervision of the Director.
(4) The objective of the Authority shall be to manage and control the use of toxic chemicals as well as to regulate the field of chemistry so as to ensure that its exclusive use is for the benefit of mankind.

provisions.title: Functions of Authority

provisions.chapter: Part III Establishment and membership of Board
provisions.title: Establishment and membership of Board

provisions.chapter: Part III Establishment and membership of Board
provisions.title: Functions of Board

definitions.definition: the Chemical Weapons Management Authority established under section 3
the Board established under section 5 of the Act

the Convention on the Prohibition, Production, Stockpiling and Use of Chemical Weapons and on their Destruction, signed by the State Parties at Paris, France on the 13th day of January, 1993, as amended from time to time
the Director of the Authority appointed under section 3 (2)
any equipment, including any building or vessel housing the equipment, where at any stage in the production of chemicals, the material flows would contain any of the scheduled chemicals
a person appointed as such under section 21
a person to whom a licence has been issued under this Act
a member of the Board appointed under section 5
a State that is not a signatory to the Convention
the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons established under the Convention
any chemical reactant which is used in the production of a chemical weapon or any toxic chemical
any chemical not listed in a Schedule, which can produce rapidly in humans sensory irritation or disabling physical effects disappear within a short time following termination of exposure
a chemical listed in Schedule 1 to this Act
a chemical listed in Schedule 2 to this Act
a chemical listed in Schedule 3 to this Act
a chemical that is listed in the Schedules to this Act
a State that is a signatory to the Convention
any chemical which through its chemical action on life processes can cause death, temporary incapacitation or permanent harm to humans or animals.
